top_stories.title: Palo Alto Networks integrates CyberArk, Koi and Portkey and launches Idira to bolster AI and identity security
top_stories.summary: Palo Alto Networks completed the acquisition of CyberArk in February 2026 and began integrating CyberArk's privileged access management capabilities into its Cortex and Strata platforms. The integration focuses on strengthening identity verification for AI agents, alongside additional acquisitions of Koi and Portkey and the launch of a new product called Idira.

top_stories.title: Okta extends AI agent security to Amazon Bedrock, opens platform to rival identity providers
top_stories.summary: Okta expanded its Okta for AI Agents platform to support any agent ecosystem, enterprise resource, and identity provider, including a new integration with Amazon Bedrock. The platform update notably opens support to rival identity providers, broadening the scope of Okta's AI agent security offering beyond its own ecosystem.

top_stories.title: Tycoon2FA hijacks Microsoft 365 accounts via device-code phishing
top_stories.summary: The Tycoon2FA phishing kit has been updated to support device-code phishing attacks targeting Microsoft 365 accounts. The kit abuses Trustifi click-tracking URLs as part of its attack chain to bypass authentication and hijack accounts.

top_stories.title: The Next Cybersecurity Challenge May Be Verifying AI Agents
top_stories.summary: The article addresses the emerging cybersecurity challenge of verifying and establishing trusted identities for AI agents operating within enterprise environments. It covers the need for identity standards, authentication mechanisms, and runtime controls specific to non-human AI identities.

top_stories.title: Palo Alto Networks Idira: A next-gen platform for human, agentic & machine identity security
top_stories.summary: Palo Alto Networks has unveiled Idira, a new trademarked next-generation identity security platform designed for human, agentic, and machine identity security. The platform is positioned around AI-era identity challenges, addressing multiple identity types within a unified offering.

news.title: Earbud sensors can authenticate users by their heartbeat, study finds
news.summary: Researchers developed AccLock, a continuous authentication system that uses accelerometer sensors in existing wireless earbuds to identify users by the vibrations their heartbeat creates in the ear canal. The system is designed to provide ongoing verification of user identity after the initial device unlock, requiring no additional hardware beyond what is already present in many earbuds.

news.title: 'Claw Chain' Vulnerabilities Threaten OpenClaw Deployments
news.summary: Patched vulnerabilities dubbed "Claw Chain" in the OpenClaw AI agent framework allowed attackers to steal credentials, escalate privileges, and maintain persistence. The flaws affected what is described as a rapidly growing AI agent framework, with the issues now resolved through patches.

news.title: Developer Workstations Are Now Part of the Software Supply Chain
news.summary: Three coordinated supply chain attack campaigns targeted npm, PyPI, and Docker Hub within a 48-hour window, focusing on stealing developer credentials and secrets. The attacks specifically targeted API keys, cloud credentials, SSH keys, and tokens stored in developer environments and CI/CD pipelines.

news.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, SAP, VMware, n8n Patch RCE, SQL Injection, Privilege Escalation Flaws
news.summary: Ivanti, Fortinet, SAP, VMware, and n8n have released security patches addressing vulnerabilities including authentication bypass and privilege escalation flaws. The most critical issue is CVE-2026-8043 (CVSS 9.6) affecting Ivanti Xtraction, which could lead to information disclosure or client-side attacks.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud credentials and internal system details in a public GitHub repository until recently. The leaked files included information on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, representing a significant government secrets exposure incident.

news.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller Auth Bypass Actively Exploited to Gain Admin Access
news.summary: Cisco has released patches for a maximum-severity authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-20182, CVSS 10.0) in Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and SD-WAN Manager that is being actively exploited. The flaw affects peering authentication mechanisms, allowing attackers to gain administrative access to affected systems.

news.title: Regional routing for AWS access portals: Implementing custom vanity domains for IAM Identity Center
news.summary: AWS has launched multi-Region replication for IAM Identity Center, enabling customers to implement custom vanity domains with regional routing for their access portals. The feature allows workforce users to access a single portal for AWS accounts and applications with improved regional resilience and customization options.

news.title: Identity breaches are on the rise, and are only getting worse - so how can your business stay safe?
news.summary: CyberArk has issued a warning about rising identity breaches, highlighting the risks posed by AI agents accessing sensitive files. The company advocates for a unified, automated identity security approach to address the growing threat landscape.

news.title: Interview: AI optimism and upskilling for a shifting job market
news.summary: Okta's chief marketing officer Shannon Duffy shared views on AI's potential as a productivity booster and job market equalizer in an interview. The discussion centers on AI optimism and workforce upskilling rather than Okta's IAM products or services.

news.title: Most Organizations Now Use AI Agents for Sensitive Security Tasks
news.summary: A Semperis study found that most organizations are now deploying AI agents for sensitive security tasks, with 74% believing AI will increase attacks on identity infrastructure. The research highlights growing concerns about AI's impact on identity security as organizations simultaneously adopt AI for security operations.

news.title: New Semperis Study Reveals AI's Effects on the Identity Attack Surface
news.summary: Semperis released a study examining how AI agents are expanding the identity attack surface, particularly around Active Directory, Entra ID, and Okta. The research finds organizations are granting AI agents access to critical systems before adequate security safeguards are in place.

news.title: Worm Redux: Fresh Mini Shai-Hulud Infections Bite Supply Chain
news.summary: Hundreds of npm packages in the TanStack open source ecosystem have been infected by a self-propagating, credential-stealing worm attributed to TeamPCP. The worm spreads through supply chain infection and is designed to steal credentials from affected systems.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Buchanan admitted involvement in SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in theft of tens of millions in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, relying solely on token theft to silently compromise user authentication at scale.
